title: Ditassa megapotamica
taxonomy: Ditassa megapotamica is classified as a species within the genus Ditassa and the family Apocynaceae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants in the same family. The Apocynaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that includes many species commonly known as dogbanes, oleanders, and periwinkles. The genus Ditassa is a small group of plants within the Apocynaceae family, primarily found in South America. By understanding its classification, researchers can better comprehend the evolutionary history and ecological roles of Ditassa megapotamica.
